Title: Structural Insights into the Catalytic Mechanism of a Plant Diterpene Glycosyltransferase SrUGT76G1

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves engineered production system by regenerating UDP-glucose needed for glycosylation steps during rebaudioside M manufacture
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway assembly in E. coli; fermentation and HPLC product analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtSUS3 was incorporated to regenerate UDP-glucose in the engineered E. coli system. 2. It supported two-step glycosylation from rebaudioside A toward rebaudioside M. 3. Its role is supportive rather than the main mechanistic focus of the paper.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful auxiliary gene for metabolic engineering platforms requiring efficient UDP-glucose supply
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a native steviol glycoside pathway structural gene in Stevia; used as cofactor-regeneration support module in heterologous production.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: 1,3-glucosylation/branch sugar chain formation in steviol glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Steviol glycosides bearing at least one glucosyl on C13-hydroxyl or C4/C19-carboxyl, including steviolmonoside, steviolbioside, rubusoside, rebaudioside A, rebaudioside D, rebaudioside E, rebaudioside G; UDP-glucose donor
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 1,3-glucosylated steviol glycosides including rebaudioside B and rebaudioside M
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to steviol glycoside acceptors to form a beta-1,3-O-glucosidic branch on existing glucosyl moieties, including conversion of steviolbioside to rebaudioside B and rebaudioside D to rebaudioside M
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target products and branch-chain sweeteners
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: High-intensity natural sweetener of economic importance
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzymatic assays; heterologous bioconversion in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes key 1,3-glucosylation reactions in steviol glycoside biosynthesis; broad substrate promiscuity enables formation of multiple steviol glycosides and is essential for rebaudioside M production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: X-ray crystal structures with ligands; in vitro enzyme assays; kinetic analysis; site-directed mutagenesis; heterologous production in E. coli; HPLC metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SrUGT76G1 is a key diterpene glycosyltransferase catalyzing multiple 1,3-glucosylation reactions in steviol glycoside biosynthesis. 2. The enzyme has a spacious acceptor pocket explaining broad specificity toward glucosylated ent-kaurene diterpenoids. 3. It does not accept non-glycosylated steviol or kaurenoic acid, indicating requirement for pre-existing sugar moieties. 4. Structural and mutational analyses identified residues controlling diterpenoid versus flavonoid recognition and product selectivity. 5. SrUGT76G1 participates in the major route to generate the high-value sweetener rebaudioside M.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for branch sugar chain formation and rebaudioside M production
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Wild-type enzyme generates side product rebaudioside I during bioconversion from rebaudioside A; broad promiscuity can create mixed product spectra
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for structure-guided glycoengineering and synthetic biology to improve steviol glycoside composition, especially increasing rebaudioside M and diversifying diterpenoid glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic base His25 and residues Thr146, Ser147, His155, Thr284, Leu126, Leu379, Gly87, Leu204, Ile199, Leu200, Leu85, Met88, Ile90, Ile203 contribute to substrate recognition or product selectivity; accepts many small molecules beyond steviol glycosides.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: 1,2-glucosylation step upstream of rebaudioside M formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 1,2-glucosylation converting rebaudioside A to rebaudioside D
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream of SrUGT76G1-mediated step in rebaudioside M route
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of rebaudioside M
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies rebaudioside D precursor required for two-step conversion from rebaudioside A to rebaudioside M in engineered E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous pathway assembly in E. coli; HPLC product analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EUGT11 catalyzes rebaudioside A to rebaudioside D conversion by 1,2-glucosylation. 2. Co-expression with SrUGT76G1 enabled two-step production of rebaudioside M from rebaudioside A. 3. It serves as the immediate upstream enzyme in the engineered RebM production route.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in gene stacking with SrUGT76G1 and UDP-glucose regeneration modules for microbial or plant engineering of rebaudioside M
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as part of the engineered dual UGT cassette for RebM manufacture rather than the primary structural subject of the paper.
